name: happyflow-generator
description: Automatically generate and execute Python test scripts from OpenAPI specifications and GraphQL schemas with enhanced features
capabilities: Parse OpenAPI 3.0+ and GraphQL specifications from URLs or files
Analyze endpoint dependencies and determine safe execution order
Generate complete Python test scripts with proper authentication handling
Execute generated scripts with automatic retry and error recovery
Support parallel execution of independent endpoints for faster testing
Validate responses against JSON Schema specifications
author: Chaim12345
securitySummary: Evaluated 81 static findings from the automated scanner. The skill is a legitimate API testing automation tool. All command execution is for running generated test scripts with hardcoded arguments. Network access fetches API specifications and makes test requests as documented. Environment variable access is for configuration only. The critical heuristic finding combines expected behaviors for API testing tools and is not malicious.
valueStatement: Manually writing API tests for complex endpoints with dependencies is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automatically generates executable Python test scripts from OpenAPI or GraphQL specifications that run endpoints in the correct dependency order.
userTitle: Generate API Test Scripts from OpenAPI Specifications
limitations: Cannot test APIs that require complex multi-step authentication flows beyond OAuth2 client credentials
Generated tests use simplified example data that may not cover all validation rules
Cannot handle websockets or real-time streaming APIs
Rate limiting handling is basic and may need customization for aggressive rate limits

useCases.targetUser: API developers needing quick validation of new endpoints
useCases.title: Quick API smoke testing
useCases.description: Generate a quick smoke test suite for a new API by providing the OpenAPI specification URL. The skill creates a script that hits all endpoints in dependency order and reports any failures.

useCases.targetUser: DevOps engineers setting up automated testing pipelines
useCases.title: CI/CD integration test generation
useCases.description: Generate a comprehensive test suite for integration testing in CI/CD pipelines. The skill produces a Python script that can be committed to the repository and run with pytest or as a standalone script.

useCases.targetUser: QA engineers verifying external API contracts
useCases.title: Third-party API testing
useCases.description: Test third-party API integrations by providing their public OpenAPI specification. Verify that the API behaves as documented and catch breaking changes early.

promptTemplates.title: Basic OpenAPI test generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the HappyFlow Generator skill to generate API tests for https://api.example.com/openapi.json with bearer token authentication. Generate a complete Python script that tests all endpoints in the correct order.
promptTemplates.scenario: Simple API test generation with bearer authentication

promptTemplates.title: GraphQL schema testing
promptTemplates.prompt: Use HappyFlow Generator to create test scripts for a GraphQL API at https://api.example.com/graphql. Include authentication and generate a script that verifies the schema works correctly.

promptTemplates.title: Parallel execution testing
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate API tests with parallel execution enabled for the OpenAPI specification at {spec_url}. Use the dependency analysis to identify which endpoints can run concurrently. Include detailed reporting.
promptTemplates.scenario: Fast parallel API testing with detailed metrics

promptTemplates.title: Authenticated API with error recovery
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a test script for the API at {spec_url} using OAuth2 client credentials with client_id={client_id}, client_secret={secret}, and token_url={token_url}. Enable automatic retry with 5 attempts and detailed reporting.
promptTemplates.scenario: OAuth2 authenticated API with retry logic

bestPractices: Always review generated scripts before running against production APIs to ensure test data is appropriate
Use separate API tokens for testing rather than production credentials to avoid unintended side effects
Set appropriate timeouts and retry counts based on the target API response times and rate limits
antiPatterns: Running generated tests directly on production APIs without reviewing the test data first
Using real user credentials for automated testing which could create unintended data or actions
Expecting the skill to understand business logic validation rules that are not in the OpenAPI specification

faq.question: What authentication methods does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports bearer tokens, API keys, Basic authentication, and OAuth2 client credentials. For other authentication types, you may need to manually add headers to the generated script.

faq.question: Can I use this with private API specifications?
faq.answer: Yes, you can provide authenticated URLs or local file paths to private specifications. The skill will use any provided credentials for fetching the specification file.

faq.question: How does dependency ordering work?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes path parameters and HTTP methods to determine which endpoints create data needed by others. It uses topological sorting to ensure creating endpoints run before endpoints that need their output.

faq.question: What happens if a test fails?
faq.answer: The skill attempts automatic fixes for common errors like missing fields or constraint violations. For complex failures, the script exits with a non-zero code and reports which step failed.

faq.question: Can I customize the generated test data?
faq.answer: Yes, you can modify the generated script to use custom test data. The skill generates example data from OpenAPI schemas, but you should replace sensitive or specific values as needed.

faq.question: Is this safe to run in CI/CD pipelines?
faq.answer: Yes, the generated scripts are standalone Python files that can be committed to version control and run in any CI/CD environment with Python and requests library installed.
